### Post by ajmorris on 2007-07-07
the -c unknown option error is normal.... but the other emerald errors, i have seen happen because this command has not been run : sudo nvidia-xconfig --add-argb-glx-visuals -d 24 , however you manually added to your xorg.conf file the result of this command..... what happens when you run compiz like this : ```
compiz --sm-diable ccp
```
and then start emerald after that if it does not start automatically.


also have you tried commenting out the dual monitor entries and running without dual monitors?

---

### Post by bodhi.zazen on 2007-07-07
Hi xpod ;)

Yea, the glib error is a little different. It is a bug and there are threads on Launchpad :

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/glib2.0/+bug/116870](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/glib2.0/+bug/116870)

### Post by ajmorris on 2007-07-08
> **bodhi.zazen said:**
> 
Compiz 0.5.1

Emerald 0.2.1

Thank you for your efforts ;)

I believe thats your problem...... maybe emerald from the repos doesnt work?.....

i have Compiz 0.5.1 (from gutsy repos)
but emerald 0.3.0 from GIT

maybe try the git version of emerald....

```
git clone git://anongit.opencompositing.org/fusion/decorators/emerald
git clone git://anongit.opencompositing.org/fusion/decorators/emerald-themes
```

and run the auto compilation scripts in the folders that it downloads (with a folder prefix as the default doesnt install to the right place) :

```
./autogen.sh --prefix=/usr/local --enable-librsvg --disable-kde && make && sudo make install
```

I hope this works for you...

---
